name: 2010 Iran Nuclear Scientist Assassinations
description: On 29 November 2010, two coordinated car-bombing attacks in Tehran targeted Iranian nuclear scientists. [Majid Shahriari](/people/majid-shahriari), a nuclear physicist at Shahid Beheshti University, was killed when a bomb attached to his car by a motorcyclist detonated; his wife survived. In a separate simultaneous attack, [Fereydoun Abbasi-Davani](/people/fereydoun-abbasi-davani) and his wife survived a similar bomb attack. The attacks were part of a broader campaign of targeted killings against Iranian nuclear scientists between 2010 and 2012 that also included the killing of Daryoush Rezaei (July 2011) and Mostafa Ahmadi Roshan (January 2012). Western and Israeli governments did not publicly claim responsibility, but the operations are widely attributed to Israeli intelligence (Mossad), possibly with US knowledge. Iran accused the United States and Israel and mourned Shahriari as a martyr of science.

sources.title: Assassination of Iranian nuclear scientists — Wikipedia
Iran Says Nuclear Scientist Killed in Explosion

editorial_notes: Part of the broader 2010–2012 assassination campaign. Widely attributed to Mossad. Shahriari killed; Abbasi-Davani survived.
